detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the British TV series âBlack Mirrorâ represent forms of control in contemporary US society?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently drives the structure of the essay.
The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with clear purpose and focus reinforced in each chapter.
Every chapter maintains a strong link between the narrative of the episodes and real-world U.S. phenomena, demonstrating effective alignment with the investigationâs aim.
weaknesses: The methodology is only implicitly described, lacking a dedicated section justifying source selection and data-gathering procedures.
Occasional overlong plot summaries momentarily detract from analytical focus.
Chapter conclusions sometimes fail to explicitly reconnect findings to the precise wording of the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission excels in articulating a sharply focused research question and maintaining alignment between each analytical chapter and the investigationâs aim, meriting near-top marks for communication and focus. However, the absence of an explicit methodology section and the uneven treatment of source-selection criteria prevent a maximum score, placing the criterion firmly at 5 out of 6.

strengths: Key academic terms (e.g. âcharismatic leadership,â âgig economyâ) are defined and applied accurately, showing solid command of subject terminology.
Primary and secondary source materials are generally relevant and integrated to support the analysis.
Subject knowledge of both Black Mirror narratives and corresponding sociopolitical concepts is coherent and well organized.
weaknesses: Application of some sources is uneven, with occasional loose connections and superficial integration of newspaper articles and handouts.
Theoretical frameworks (e.g. panopticism, surveillance capitalism) are under-utilized, limiting conceptual depth.
Citations exhibit inconsistencies and incomplete details, undermining the overall scholarly rigour.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a generally clear and coherent understanding of relevant concepts and mostly appropriate use of sources, warranting a solid mid-band score of 4. Inconsistent source application and limited theoretical engagement, however, prevent it from reaching the top band.

strengths: Arguments are logically structured, with clear thematic links between episodes and real-world examples.
Each chapter contains a concise mini-conclusion that reinforces the overarching argument.
Evidence from fictional texts and secondary literature is consistently employed to support key claims.
weaknesses: Critical evaluation is limited; counterarguments and alternative perspectives receive only brief acknowledgement.
Source quality is mixed, with reliance on non-academic materials (blogs, handouts) diluting the rigour of the research.
Some inferential leaps (e.g. direct analogies between cycling and gig work) lack empirical backing and weaken analysis depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay achieves good coherence and relevance in its critical discussion, aligning with the mid-band descriptors for Critical Thinking. However, only partial engagement with counterarguments, mixed source quality, and occasional unsupported inferences limit its score to 4 out of 12 in proportion to the criterionâs weighting.

strengths: All major structural elements are present (title page, abstract/introduction, labelled chapters, conclusion, bibliography).
The table of contents accurately reflects section headings, aiding navigation.
Consistent styling of chapter headings supports reader orientation.
weaknesses: Page numbers are entirely missing, undermining the documentâs navigability.
Footnote formatting and bibliographic entries are inconsistent in style and placement.
Some required layout conventions (e.g., uniform footnote indentation) are not applied correctly.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the extended essayâs overall structure is coherent and complete, significant layout lapsesâmost notably the absence of page numbers and inconsistent citation formattingâplace the work at the lower end of the 1â2 band, justifying a score of 2 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths** â The research questionââTo what extent does the British TV series Black Mirror represent forms of control in contemporary US society?ââis prominently stated on the title page and repeated in the introduction. It is sharply phrased, clearly delimited (three specified episodes; political, economic and social control; contemporary US context) and drives the structure of the discussion.   

Each analytical chapter explicitly returns to the wording of the question, culminating in a conclusion that synthesises findings in direct response to âto what extentâ. This sustained linkage shows an effective connection between the question and the ensuing discussion.   

**Weaknesses** â Minor conceptual slippages (e.g., labelling PRISM as a âcompanyâ) indicate slight unevenness in focus, yet these do not undermine the overall clarity. The question remains consistently and effectively foregrounded, meriting the top band.

**Strengths** â Each chapter progresses from narrative summary to thematic comparison, offering clear analytical links between fictional scenarios and US socio-political realities. The student evaluates charismatic rhetoric, labour precarity and data surveillance, drawing logical parallels that generally support the central conclusion.   

**Weaknesses** â Analysis sometimes rests on oversimplified equivalences (e.g., treating bicycle pedalling as a direct analogue to Uber driving) or unexamined assumptions (all surveillance equals control). Counter-arguments and limitations are acknowledged only briefly, and some inferential steps lack evidential backing.   

Nevertheless, argumentative coherence outweighs these lapses; depth is âgood with minor inconsistencies,â situating the work mid-band with a score of 5.

**Strengths** â Primary sources (the three episodes) are described accurately, and secondary materialsâjournal articles on charisma, gig-economy reports, surveillance studiesâare generally relevant to the arguments. Quotations from both episodes and scholarship are integrated to illustrate claims (e.g., slogans from Waldo; academic definitions of bandwagon propaganda).   

**Weaknesses** â Application is uneven: some citations (e.g., Johns Hopkins handout, newspaper articles) are only loosely connected, and key assertions (e.g., equating PRISM with a commercial entity) lack precise sourcing. Engagement with opposing viewpoints or methodological studies is minimal, and several references appear only superficially.   

Overall, the majority of sources are relevant but not always critically or consistently employed, placing the strand in the 3â4 band at the lower end.

**Strengths** â The document displays a title page, word count, and uniform font, and headings are clearly distinguished from body text.   

**Weaknesses** â A critical layout elementâpage numberingâis entirely missing, meaning the table of contents cannot direct the reader accurately. Footnote indentation and font size vary, and some bibliographic entries are inconsistent in style. These faults indicate that some layout conventions are present but others are incorrect or absent, placing the work at the bottom of the 1â2 band.

**Strengths** â The student demonstrates solid familiarity with Black Mirror narratives and with key sociopolitical concepts such as charismatic leadership, gig-economy labour precarity, and surveillance capitalism. Explanations of episodes are clear, and connections to US contexts (Trumpâs rhetoric, Uber labour model, Facebook/PRISM) show coherent subject understanding.   

**Weaknesses** â Depth of theoretical engagement is limited; discussions sometimes default to plot retelling rather than sustained scholarly analysis. Certain generalisations (e.g., âtechnology creates a form of modern slaveryâ) are asserted rather than substantiated with rigorous evidence.   

While knowledge is generally accurate and organised, the partial reliance on anecdotal or popular sources prevents the leap to the highest band. Hence, a solid position in the 3â4 range is justified.

**Strengths** â The essay includes all major structural elements required by the IB (title page, abstract/introduction, clearly labelled chapters, conclusion, bibliography). The table of contents corresponds to section headings, and chapter sequencing is logical, facilitating reader navigation.   

**Weaknesses** â Minor inconsistencies in heading styles and the placement of footnotes do not significantly impede comprehension. Overall, structure remains coherent and complete, matching the top band for this criterion.

**Strengths** â The essay integrates around fifteen secondary sources, several of which are peer-reviewed articles in political science or surveillance studies, demonstrating adequate breadth. The chosen episodes and real-world cases relate clearly to the research question, and sources are generally cited to support points.   

**Weaknesses** â Source quality is mixed; the inclusion of blog posts, newspaper articles and a university writing-centre PDF dilutes academic rigour, and the bibliography shows inconsistency and some incomplete references. The absence of source evaluation or triangulation limits confidence in the researchâs reliability.   

Consequently the research is best described as adequate and partially relevant, corresponding to the 4â6 band but nearer its lower boundary.

**Strengths** â The studentâs topicâhow Black Mirror represents forms of political, economic and social control in contemporary US societyâis articulated unambiguously on the title page and reiterated in the introduction. Purpose and focus are reinforced in each chapter opening and in the conclusion, ensuring the reader is constantly reminded of the guiding idea.   

The work remains tightly anchored to the stated aim: every chapter begins with a synopsis of the chosen episode and immediately links the narrative to a real
-world US phenomenon (Trumpâs populism, the gig economy, PRISM/Facebook). This repeated alignment shows a clear, appropriate focus that fits the subject requirements for an English B Category 2 essay.   

**Weaknesses** â A few sections drift into extended plot summary before returning to analysis, but these moments do not fundamentally blur the essayâs overall purpose. Overall clarity and appropriateness remain strong, warranting the top band.

**Strengths** â The discussion is well-organised: each chapter ends with a concise mini-conclusion that reinforces the overarching argument, and the final conclusion synthesises findings in direct response to the research question. Reasoning is generally logical and supported by examples from both text and scholarship.   

**Weaknesses** â Evaluation is limited; potential benefits or counter-perspectives (e.g., flexibility within gig work, positive dimensions of social media) are noted but not interrogated. Limitations, reliability of evidence, and implications for further research are largely absent, reducing the overall critical weight.   

As a result, the strand fits the description of âgoodâ discussion with identifiable weaknesses, justifying a score of 4 in the 4â6 band.

**Strengths** â The student explains that three episodes will be compared with specific US phenomena and provides a bibliographic list containing academic articles, news sources and primary texts. This indicates some informed source selection and a basic methodological plan anchored in comparative textual analysis.   

**Weaknesses** â The methodology section is implicit rather than explicit: there is no dedicated discussion of source selection criteria, data-gathering procedures, or limitations. Popular media items (e.g., newspaper pieces, writing-centre handout) sit alongside peer-reviewed scholarship without justification of their relative weight or reliability. Citations occasionally lack full details, and the absence of a systematic evaluative rationale means the methodology is only partially complete.   

Given these gaps, the work fits the 3â4 band: there is evidence of some informed selection but not a fully articulated or consistently applied method.

**Strengths** â Key terms such as âcharismatic leadership,â âbandwagon propaganda,â âgig economy,â and the âbourgeois notion of privacyâ are defined accurately and used repeatedly, indicating a competent command of academic vocabulary. Conceptual explanations (e.g., defining gig economy as short-term contract labour) aid reader understanding.   

**Weaknesses** â Terminology is occasionally applied loosely (e.g., confusing PRISMâs governmental nature, inconsistent spelling of âprosumptionâ). Some core concepts from media or cultural theory that would deepen analysisâsuch as ideology, hegemony, or panopticismâare mentioned little or not at all, limiting precision and consistency.   

On balance the terminology is mostly accurate and generally consistent, fulfilling the descriptors for the 3â4 band at its upper end.

annotations.comment: Making explicit links between bandwagon propaganda and the research question enhances argumentative coherence and demonstrates effective analytical reasoning.
The chapter heading is clearly formatted and consistent with earlier sections, reinforcing a coherent organizational structure.
Primary episode details are accurately recounted but lack inâtext references to distinguish between student analysis and source material effectively.
The surveillance discussion lacks reference to theoretical frameworks such as the Panopticon or surveillance capitalism, which would deepen the conceptual analysis.
The clear and consistent styling of this chapter header supports reader orientation and contributes positively to overall layout design.
The critique of Facebookâs privacy issues is valid but would benefit from engagement with arguments that highlight user agency or dataâsharing benefits.
Footnote entries vary in style and punctuation; adopting a single citation style throughout the essay would improve professionalism and consistency.
Drawing parallels between the episode and PRISM is effective, but acknowledging methodological limitations (e.g., lack of legal analysis) would strengthen credibility.
The conclusion succinctly summarizes key findings but stops short of reflecting on methodological choices or the research process itself, limiting reflective insight.
Bibliography entries are inconsistently formatted (italicization and ordering); applying a standard citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) throughout would ensure uniformity.
Methodological planning is described implicitly; the essay lacks a dedicated methodology section that justifies source selection and outlines dataâgathering procedures.
This transitional paragraph reiterates plot details without integrating analytical insight; the student should interweave critical commentary earlier to maintain momentum.
The concept of âdynamic leadershipâ is introduced without citation of an academic definition or theoretical framework, weakening the conceptual foundation.
The research question is prominently stated and tightly focused, effectively guiding the essayâs structure and maintaining alignment with the investigationâs aim.
Including the word count on the title page meets the IB formatting requirement and demonstrates awareness of the investigationâs scope.
The inclusion of a table of contents enhances navigability and reflects a coherent structural design for the extended essay.
Although a table of contents is present, the absence of visible page numbering in the document undermines its functionality and should be corrected.
Defining the âgig economyâ via a standalone footnote is weak; a peerâreviewed source would provide greater credibility and precision.
The metaphor relating cycling to Uber driving is insightful but would be strengthened by incorporating empirical data on gigâeconomy working hours.
The chapter conclusion is concise but does not explicitly reconnect the findings to the precise wording of the research question, missing an opportunity for synthesis.
The title is clearly stated at the start of the report, fulfilling the requirement for clear identification of the investigationâs focus.
The introductory paragraph provides general context but remains too broad and offers limited linkage to the specific research question, diluting the essayâs initial focus.
This section heavily summarizes narrative events; to deepen analysis, the student should balance plot description with sustained critical evaluation of its significance.
The footnote styling is inconsistent (use of horizontal rule and spacing); aligning footnotes with one consistent format would improve presentation.
The analysis of charismatic leadership would benefit from engagement with contrasting scholarly perspectives to demonstrate critical depth and broaden understanding.
The duplicate use of the horizontal rule before this footnote is unnecessary and distracts from the flow; streamline footnote formatting for clarity.
The concluding statement of this chapter lacks consideration of counterarguments on the efficacy of propaganda, limiting the evaluationâs critical balance.
The extensive summary of âFifteen Million Meritsâ needs tighter integration with theoretical insights on digital labor to advance scholarly relevance.
This synthesis is coherent but remains descriptive; incorporating deeper theoretical evaluation of privacy concepts would enhance scholarly rigor.
The final bibliographic entry omits volume and issue details, reducing reference completeness; verify and include all publication information for accuracy.
